headline: Chip bull market cracking — trader concern rising
detail: A direct warning that the chip stock rally is showing stress, with traders actively discussing protection strategies. Your book is 90% tech/chip names on margin, so a sector-wide rotation or selloff hits you harder than almost anyone. SMH, AVGO, and CRWD are all directly exposed.

headline: Everyone buying the dip — contrarian red flag
detail: When dip-buying becomes consensus, history says that's exactly when the next leg down does real damage because there's no fresh buying left. You're already holding several underwater positions on margin, meaning a failed dip-buy scenario could accelerate losses beyond your stops.

headline: Tech stocks leading this bull market far more volatile than old guard
detail: The article flags that the names driving recent gains — exactly the kind of high-beta tech you hold — carry significantly more downside velocity than traditional leaders. On margin, that volatility isn't just uncomfortable, it's a margin-call risk if multiple names drop simultaneously.

headline: Index funds hiding looming tech-stock risk
detail: Broad index exposure like your SPY and QQQ positions may be masking concentrated tech risk that looks diversified but isn't. Worth noting your QQQ and SPY are both slightly underwater and their stops at $670 and $705 are meaningful levels to watch if tech pressure continues.
